title: Fable Anniversary

notes: In order to get it running, you either need to use winetricks or protontricks and install dotnet40, and without gallium-nine enabled the game has a few graphical hiccups, however after enabling gallium-nine (works on the open-source amd graphics driver only) the game will run at perfect 60fps with seemingly no graphical issues.
title: Fable Anniversary

notes: After the dotnet40 trick the game launched and has fairly stable framerate. Some texture tearing and black textures though.

notes: This game is running out of the box. I did see some minor screen glitches but the game was perfectly playable!

notes: After using protontricks to reinstall dotnet 4.0, and installing winbind, I was able to get it up and going. There is a lot of issues with the way the graphics are rendering (ie... the eyes on are all black, the book looks muted). It is playable, but annoying.

title: Fable Anniversary

notes: Black textures are fixed with d9vk (https://github.com/Joshua-Ashton/d9vk). It now runs perfectly, no more dark eyes!
title: Fable Anniversary

notes: Installed dv9k as suggested by another report (https://github.com/Joshua-Ashton/d9vk) which solved black eyes missing textures and some screen flickering. It runs full screen and windowed, after installing d9vk to the proton wine prefix for it without issues.
title: Fable Anniversary

notes: The game launches, but before the window loads, crashes, forcing the user to restart Steam. This is with the fixes and tweaks which work for other users.
title: Fable Anniversary

notes: the graphics issues can be solved by installing d9vk.  Look here for reference on installing it. https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/1542 Unfortunately after a certain point the game seems to randomly crash.
title: Fable Anniversary

notes: D9vk is not needed anymore to fix the black textures (but you can enable it, it's probably better).
title: Fable Anniversary

notes:
I played the game using Proton 4.2-9, but the last post intrigued me, so I tried out 4.11-2 as well. 4.11-2 does fix the black textures, but it doesn't fix the artifacts and introduces lag spikes. Just use 4.2-9 and add d9vk (https://github.com/Joshua-Ashton/d9vk/releases). Download the latest .tar.gz, extract it, and within the newly extracted directory, run these commands (without the square brackets):

Command #1: [ export WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/debian-installation/steamapps/compatdata/288470/pfx ] 
Command #2: [ ./setup_dxvk.sh install ]

Just make sure that you've run the game once and have Wine configured (by running [ winecfg ]) beforehand. After that, it runs perfectly.
title: Fable Anniversary

notes: Runs great!! Just install 'FXAA Removal' from the Steam Workshop for a lag free experience :)
title: Fable Anniversary
